skill.name: esimaccess-api
skill.description: Manage eSIM Access API operations including balance checks, package listing, order creation, eSIM status monitoring, top-ups, cancellations, profile management, webhooks, and refund/reissue workflows. Use when user mentions eSIM, ICCID, data plan, roaming, SIM card, mobile data package, travel connectivity, cellular data usage, or interacts with the eSIM Access API at api.esimaccess.com. Also trigger when user asks about balance, top-up, order status, QR code provisioning, or uploads a list of ICCIDs or order numbers.
skill.summary: Complete eSIM lifecycle management via the eSIM Access API with 17 operations for ordering, monitoring, top-ups, and profile control.
skill.author: esimaccess

security_audit.summary: The eSIM Access API skill is a legitimate API integration tool for eSIM lifecycle management. Static analyzer flagged 239 potential issues, but AI review confirms these are all false positives. The 'external_commands' detections are markdown backtick syntax for code formatting in documentation files, not shell execution. Hardcoded URLs point to the legitimate eSIM Access API service. HMAC-SHA256 crypto usage is secure. No malicious patterns, credential exfiltration, or prompt injection detected.

low_findings.title: Documentation Uses Backtick Syntax
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' in markdown files. These are false positives - backticks are standard markdown syntax for inline code formatting (e.g., `code`). No shell commands are executed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Markdown files use backticks exclusively for code formatting syntax, not shell execution. The backticks appear in code fence blocks (```) and inline code (`variable`).

low_findings.title: Legitimate API Endpoint References
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Hardcoded URL' patterns. These are false positives - all URLs point to the legitimate eSIM Access API service (api.esimaccess.com) required for skill functionality.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All hardcoded URLs are legitimate eSIM Access API endpoints. The skill cannot function without these API references.

low_findings.title: Secure Cryptographic Implementation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Weak cryptographic algorithm'. This is a false positive - the skill uses HMAC-SHA256 which is a secure, industry-standard signing algorithm for API authentication.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: HMAC-SHA256 is FIPS 180-4 compliant and widely used for API authentication. No weak algorithms like MD5 or SHA1 are used for security-critical operations.

content.user_title: Manage eSIM Profiles via API Integration
content.value_statement: Developers need a reliable way to programmatically manage eSIM lifecycles for IoT, travel, and enterprise mobility applications. This skill provides Claude Code with complete eSIM Access API integration including ordering, monitoring, top-ups, and profile management through 17 operations with HMAC-SHA256 authentication.
actual_capabilities: Query account balance and list available data packages by country or ICCID
Create eSIM orders and poll for ICCID provisioning status
Apply data top-ups to existing eSIM profiles
Check eSIM status, data usage, and full profile details
Suspend, unsuspend, and revoke eSIM profiles
Configure webhooks for order status and usage notifications
Cancel unused eSIMs with automatic refund and reissue workflows
limitations: Requires valid accessCode from eSIM Access developer console
Cannot activate or configure eSIMs on physical devices (requires QR code scanning)
Top-up eligibility depends on package configuration and expiry status

use_cases.title: IoT Fleet Management
use_cases.description: Manage hundreds of eSIM profiles across IoT devices. Monitor data usage, apply top-ups when fleets approach limits, and suspend inactive devices to control costs.

use_cases.title: Travel Tech Application
use_cases.description: Build travel apps that automatically provision eSIMs for destinations, check roaming packages, and let users manage their data plans while abroad.

use_cases.title: Enterprise Mobility Platform
use_cases.description: Automate employee device provisioning with centralized eSIM management, real-time usage monitoring, and automated policy enforcement.

prompt_templates.title: Check Account Balance
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the esimaccess-api skill to check my account balance
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to see current balance before ordering or topping up

prompt_templates.title: Order eSIM Package
prompt_templates.prompt: Use esimaccess-api to order a 5GB 30-day plan for the United States and show me the order details and QR code
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to purchase a new eSIM for travel

prompt_templates.title: Monitor Data Usage
prompt_templates.prompt: Use esimaccess-api to check data usage for ICCID 89852240810733629810
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to track how much data an eSIM has consumed

prompt_templates.title: Top-up and Cancel Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use esimaccess-api to apply a 3GB top-up to esimTranNo 26041021130002, then check if cancellation with refund is available for esimTranNo 26041021130003
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to manage multiple eSIMs efficiently

output_examples.output: Your eSIM Access account balance is $22.30 USD. You have sufficient balance for most standard data packages (5GB plans start at $8.80).

output_examples.input: What packages are available for Japan?
output_examples.output:
Available Japan packages:
- Japan 3GB 15Days ($4.80)
- Japan 5GB 30Days ($7.20)
- Japan 10GB 30Days ($12.50)

All support top-ups. Most include 4G/LTE connectivity.

Order B26041021130002 Status:
- ICCID: 89852240810733629810
- SMDP Status: ENABLED
- eSIM Status: GOT_RESOURCE
- QR Code: https://p.qrsim.net/xxx.png

Your eSIM is ready for installation.

best_practices: Store your accessCode securely in environment variables, never commit it to version control
Use webhooks for production monitoring instead of polling, as polling has rate limits
Store esimTranNo alongside ICCID in your system - usage queries require esimTranNo
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode accessCode in scripts that will be shared or committed to repositories
Do not poll for ICCID more than every 3 seconds - the API needs time to provision
Do not attempt to cancel activated eSIMs - use revoke instead for activated profiles

faq.question: How do I get an accessCode for the eSIM Access API?
faq.answer: Register at https://console.esimaccess.com/developer/index and generate an accessCode from the developer console. The accessCode serves as both your API identifier and HMAC signing key.

faq.question: What is the difference between esimTranNo and ICCID?
faq.answer: The esimTranNo is the internal transaction ID assigned by eSIM Access. The ICCID is the SIM card serial number. You need esimTranNo for usage queries and top-ups, but ICCID is needed for QR code provisioning and SMS.

faq.question: Why did my cancel request fail?
faq.answer: Cancellation only works on unused eSIMs with status RELEASED or GOT_RESOURCE. Once an eSIM is activated (has an EID or activateTime), you must use revoke instead, which does not provide a refund.

faq.question: How do I know when my eSIM order is ready?
faq.answer: After creating an order, poll /esim/query with your orderNo every 3 seconds. Alternatively, configure a webhook with type ORDER_STATUS for automatic notifications when the ICCID becomes available.

faq.question: Can I top up an expired eSIM?
faq.answer: No, you cannot top up an expired eSIM. You must order a new eSIM. The skill will warn you if you attempt to top up an expired profile.

faq.question: What authentication does the API use?
faq.answer: The eSIM Access API uses HMAC-SHA256 authentication. Every request requires a signature computed from timestamp + requestId + accessCode + request body, signed with your accessCode as the key.
